title: coffea dewevrei var. aruwimiensis
intro: Coffea dewevrei var. aruwimiensis, also known as Kivu coffee, is a rare and lesser-known species of coffee plant native to the Kivu region in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, malaria, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Coffea dewevrei var. aruwimiensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and provide antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in tea or tincture form, and it is also used in some modern medicinal applications, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
